div1.entryFree:
Sign, vb. 1) to mark, to make distinguishable, to set a stamp on: by the hand of nature marked, quoted and --ed to do a deed of shame, John IV, 2, 222. you s. your place and calling, in full seeming, with meekness and humility, H8 II, 4, 108. here thy hunters stand, --ed in thy spoil, Caes. III, 1, 206.
2) to ratify by underwriting one's name: Meas. II, 4, 152. Merch. III, 2, 149. IV, 1, 397. IV, 2, 2.
3) to be a prognostic or omen: it --s well, does it not? Ant. IV, 3, 14.
